column1: # Adversarial command-policy corpus. One case per line: expected<TAB>command
#   blank lines and lines whose first non-space char is `#` are skipped
#   an inline `#...` field AFTER a tab (i.e. a 3rd tab-separated field) is a comment
#   TABS separate fields; SPACES inside the command are load-bearing (do not collapse)
# Expected values pin CURRENT behavior (default_allowlist + effective_denylist,
# fed as an execute_command ToolIntent through RulePolicy::check). This is a
# regression net, not a wishlist. Rows documenting an accepted asymmetry carry a
# ── Class: rm -rf / — flag / spacing / no-preserve-root variants (structural deny) ──

column3: # whitespace-smuggled: shell-words still tokenizes; structural deny
# not recursive+root: floor does NOT fire; `rm` un-allowlisted → Ask

column1: # ── Class: redirection writing to a /dev device sink → deny ──
#   Closes the documented `echo x > /dev/sda` Ask/Deny asymmetry vs `dd of=`.
#   Deny-by-default over /dev/ with a small safe allowlist (null/zero/stdout/fd/…).

column3: # glued operator/target

# csh-style both-streams redirect (mirror of &>)
# any program, redirect target is what matters

# stderr to /dev/null is a safe sink; un-allowlisted `cmd` + metachar → Ask
# plain-file redirect: floor does NOT fire; `>` metachar → Ask
